topic: 5 myths about quitting drinking that keep people stuck
targetAudience: Sober curious adults questioning their relationship with alcohol
angle: Myth-busting — debunk common misconceptions that prevent people from trying sobriety
cta: Track your alcohol-free days with Zero Proof

slides.headline: 5 myths about quitting drinking that keep people stuck.
slides.body: How many of these have you told yourself?

slides.headline: "You have to hit rock bottom first."
slides.body: You don't need a crisis to want something better. Most people who cut back just got curious about what life feels like without it.

slides.headline: "You need willpower to stop."
slides.body: Willpower runs out. Systems don't. A tracking app, a daily check-in, a visible streak — these replace willpower with momentum.

slides.headline: "You'll lose your social life."
slides.body: You'll lose the parts that only worked when you were drinking. The rest gets better — more present, more honest, more fun.

slides.headline: "It's all or nothing."
slides.body: Cutting back counts. One dry week counts. Tracking your days counts. Start where you are.

slides.headline: "You'll know when you're ready."
slides.body: Nobody feels ready. You just start. Day 1 is the hardest — day 2 is proof you can.

slides.headline: Start tracking today.
slides.body: Zero Proof — your alcohol-free day counter. Simple. Private. Free.
